name: Ears
summary: Local OBS audio/video ingest and voice-command bridge for Codex. RTMP ingest through MediaMTX, local ffmpeg audio chunking, faster-whisper transcription, and a conservative Robot wake-word command router are implemented. The router echoes interpreted commands and queues approved commands, but does not execute them automatically.
next_actions: Restart MediaMTX and OBS, then run a clean voice-command session with `Robot, check the Ears status.` followed by spoken approval.
Make router confirmations visible in the active terminal without requiring a separate manual tail command.
Add an operator-facing drain step for `/srv/cloud-mirror/Ears/commands/approved.jsonl` so approved voice commands can be reviewed and handed to Codex intentionally.
Improve command grouping across audio chunk boundaries and tune confirmation timeout/cooldown after more real use.
Add a cleanup/archive script for old processed WAV chunks and stale session artifacts.
blockers: No automatic executor is connected yet; approved voice commands are queued only.
Confirmation feedback is terminal/log based, not speaker based.
OBS must be configured to avoid desktop-audio feedback loops during command mode.
